questions.prompt: Where does the compatibility chain in part selection usually begin?
options: With goggle selection
With the frame and propeller size; this cascades into the choice of motors, battery, ESC, and FC
With random, cheapest parts
With the radio first, then everything else
questions.explanation: Everything starts with the frame (and the propeller size it dictates); from there the motors (+ battery S), ESC (current), and FC (mounting standard + UART) are chosen to be compatible.

questions.prompt: What is the most critical value when choosing an ESC?
options: Its color matching the frame
Its current capacity (amps) being higher than the peak current the motors will draw
Having the lowest possible current capacity
Being as heavy as possible
questions.explanation: The current the ESC can deliver must be higher than the motors' peak current, with headroom; a low-capacity ESC burns out (lets the smoke out) when its current limit is exceeded.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is true for a safe, good solder joint?
options: Keeping the iron as cool as possible gives a good joint
Breathing in solder fumes directly is harmless
Work in a ventilated area at the correct temperature; a good joint is shiny and concave, and you avoid a cold (dull/blobby) joint
A good joint always looks dull and blobby
questions.explanation: Soldering is done in a ventilated area at sufficient temperature. A good joint is shiny and concave; a dull/blobby cold joint is weak and can come loose in flight.

questions.prompt: When should the propellers be installed during assembly and first tests?
options: In the first step, right after the motors
Last; the first power-up and motor tests are absolutely done props-off
Right before soldering
Right before plugging in the battery, for testing
questions.explanation: Propellers go on last, and the first tests (motor direction, settings) are done props-off; an accidentally spinning propeller can seriously cut a finger.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of using a smoke-stopper when plugging in the battery for the first time?
options: To make the drone fly faster
To charge the battery faster
By limiting current, to prevent parts from burning if there's a fault like reverse polarity or a short, and to catch the fault without smoke
To sharpen the video image
questions.explanation: A smoke-stopper goes between the battery and the drone and limits the current. If there's a fault (e.g. reverse polarity), the parts give a warning instead of burning; this lets you catch an expensive mistake before the smoke comes out.
